reasoning_model_1: The model fails significantly on the first criterion: Content Accuracy. The requested text was 'I think I heard something, is someone out there? Oh my goodness, you actually prepared a birthday party for me!', but the audio generated by Model 1 cuts off abruptly after 'Oh my goodness', omitting the entire phrase 'you actually prepared a birthday party for me!'. Because the content is clearly inconsistent with the script due to a major omission, the model receives a score of 0 regardless of the emotional performance in the first half.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 follows the instructions perfectly. Content Accuracy is flawless with no omissions. Regarding Paralinguistic Compliance, the model successfully executes the dynamic adjustment. From 0:00 to 0:04 ('I think I heard something...'), the voice is breathy, hesitant, and trembling, effectively conveying fear. At 0:05 ('Oh my goodness'), there is a distinct shift in tone. From 0:06 to the end ('you actually prepared...'), the voice becomes bright, higher-pitched, and energetic, accurately conveying happy surprise. The transition is natural and fits the narrative context perfectly.
model_comparison: Model 1 failed to complete the required text, stopping halfway through the sentence. This is a critical failure in content accuracy. Model 2 not only completed the full text accurately but also demonstrated a high-quality transition from fear to surprise as requested. The fear segment in Model 2 was suitably tremulous, and the surprise segment was genuinely enthusiastic. Model 2 is the clear winner due to Model 1's incomplete generation.
